# Transcrição: 15- How to Set Up Business Manager

## [00:00 - 03:01]

This video is going to cover how to create a business manager so that you can manage all of your clients ad accounts, your clients pages and add in any contractors or team members that you have which allows you to keep everything in one hub so that it's super easy to find everything you need and makes it a lot more organized than using your personal account because if you were to have your personal account on every client's ad account and every client's page it would get very messy very quickly. So to create your business manager you need to go to businessmanagers URL which is business.facebook.com so it does have its own URL separate to Facebook and then this will be the screen that shows and you'll click create account. So in this pop-up we'll show telling you to create your business manager account and the first thing you need to do is enter in your business name so you don't need to overthink this typically this is just your agency's name so here i'm just going to type in FATC example and hit continue. So now this is you creating your profile for you to use within that business manager so it'll pre-populate with your name if you're logged into Facebook and you just need to tell it which email address you want to use so this doesn't have to be your personal email address typically what you would put in here is your business email so i'm just going to put in a dummy email address that i have at the moment and then you hit finish and it's going to take you into the main screen of business manager which looks like this so because there's no pages or no ad accounts in it yet it's telling me to add pages add ad accounts add people and there's no assets showing up here so the first thing that you need to do is add your primary page because Facebook won't let you add in any of your clients ad accounts or your clients pages until you have a primary page associated with your business manager so this helps Facebook to stop people from creating fraudulent business manager accounts or to stop bots from creating thousands of them at one time so in order to add in your primary page you need to claim your business page your business's Facebook page into business manager so to do that you need to go to business settings there's three ways that you can get to business settings the first one is this very clear blue button in the top right that says business settings you can also click on this wheel over here which is also going to take you to business settings or in the hamburger menu if you hover over all tools you'll see business settings on the very right so this is the first screen that you typically see when you go into your business manager settings it'll show the people right now there's only me and there so there's no team members or contractors yet and you've also got pages you've got ad accounts

## [03:01 - 05:44]

apps pixels catalogs and so on when you first start start out you'll primarily just need to worry about pages ad accounts and pixels but as you get more involved and as you pick up clients or start doing things that are a bit more technical then you'll start getting a bit more involved in the other categories on the list but for now just worried about the ones towards the top so the first thing that we need to do is claim that primary page into business manager so the really important thing to note about claiming a page is that once you claim it you can't unclaim so you don't want to tie in any pages to this business manager account that you don't want permanently tied to it so for example if you owned a couple of businesses you had your Facebook ads agency and you also have hair salon you wouldn't want to claim your hair salon page into business manager because if you wanted to sell your agency at some point in the future you'd probably going to sell the business manager as well because it's going to have all of your clients ad accounts and all of your clients pages and you don't want your hair salon page being tied to it as well it is okay to tie your agency's page into it though so that's what you would do here right now it's telling me that there aren't any pages in here so I'm going to go add new pages and click on this top button which is add a page and you can see that's going to claim the page and move it into business manager so to come up with this pop up here where you can type in the Facebook page name or the URL for it if you type in the name and the page doesn't show up don't be too concerned all you need to do is pull up the page in a new tab and copy the URL directly and paste that in here because sometimes Facebook doesn't recognize the name of the page if it potentially went through a page name change in the past so I'm just going to add in this dummy page right now and click add page and since I'm already an admin it's already being added into the business manager account so I'll click close on that and that is the page claimed in there you can see that it's already been assigned to me as well because I was an admin on the page before we claimed it in so you can also this is where you can add people you can add any team members up here by clicking new people but we'll go through all of that in another video but the important thing is now that we've claimed the page we can add in our clients pages and our clients add accounts and our business manager is ready to go
